title: Read-only static JSON API over the catalog (no patient data)
context: A programmatic access layer over the catalog enables downstream pipelines and the Hee-Lee Oss sibling projects (ewing-open-data-catalog, cancer-dataset-datasheets) to query the access-tier and license-rights information before touching any data. The API is served as static JSON (e.g. pre-generated endpoint files) hosted on GitHub Pages or equivalent — no server that stores user input, no patient data, no tracking. Key endpoint: GET /cohort/{id}/terms returns the access tier, license rights booleans, and provenance for a given cohort. MIT-licensed.
objective: Generate a static JSON API over the catalog records (per-cohort terms endpoints + an index) so downstream pipelines and sibling projects can programmatically query access tiers and license rights without a backend server, patient data, or tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: A build script generates static JSON API endpoint files (e.g. api/cohort/{id}/terms.json) from catalog records; the files are committed and served statically.
Each endpoint returns: id, name, accessTier, license (rights booleans with fieldProvenance references), accessTerms, recordState, and provenance.retrievedAt.
No server stores user input, no tracking, no patient data, no credentials required to read the API.
An index endpoint (api/index.json) lists all published records with their id, name, accessTier, and entryType for discovery.
The API build integrates into CI (generated at build time alongside catalog records); pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; MIT-licensed; DCO signed-off.
resources: open-cohort-catalog PLAN.md (adjacent opportunities: cross-cohort access-terms API)
output: A committed static JSON API build script (MIT) that generates per-cohort terms endpoints and an index, served statically with no backend, enabling programmatic access-tier querying by adopters.
